popup.version_too_old: This antd version is too old. The extension needs {min} or newer.
popup.prerelease_warning: This is a pre-release antd build ({version}). The extension supports stable releases only.
popup.disconnected_help: The extension needs the Autonomi daemon (antd) to fetch content from the network.
popup.then_detect_prefix: Then click Detect daemon above, or

guide.summary: Already installed antd? Find & run it
guide.reach_antd: If you set up antd before, it may simply be stopped — or running without the option the extension needs. The extension reaches antd at {url} and needs it started with the --cors option.

terminal.linux: press Ctrl+Alt+T (or open your terminal app)

guide.cmd_not_found: Keep that window open while you browse. If you see “command not found”, antd isn't on your PATH — run it using the full path shown below (still adding --cors).
guide.path_varies: varies by platform — see the setup guide
guide.portfile_hint: — this file appears once antd has started.
guide.connects_automatically: Once it's running, this page connects automatically — or click Detect daemon in the toolbar popup.

install.step_run: Run the installer that just downloaded ({file}).
install.step2_install: It starts antd and sets it to launch on login.
install.step2_update: It replaces your current antd and restarts it.
install.step_detected: This panel updates automatically once antd is detected.

onboarding.welcome_lede: You're almost ready to browse content from the Autonomi network. One quick setup step and you're in.
onboarding.step1_body: Autonomi content lives on a decentralized network. A small local program called antd runs on your computer and fetches that content for the extension — your browser can't reach the network on its own. It runs quietly in the background and only listens on your own machine.
onboarding.step2_body: Open the file you just downloaded and follow the prompts. It installs antd, starts it, and sets it to launch automatically each time you sign in — so you only do this once.
onboarding.step3_title: Check the extension for a connection
onboarding.step3_body: This page detects the daemon automatically. Once it's running, the banner above turns green and you're connected to the network. You can also click the Autonomi icon in your browser toolbar any time to see your status.
onboarding.done_body: The extension can now load Autonomi content. Visit a page with autonomi:// references, or open the toolbar popup to see what's detected.
onboarding.os_undetected: We couldn't detect your operating system — pick the right build on GitHub.
onboarding.download_failed_fallback: Download failed — opening the GitHub releases page instead.
onboarding.banner_idle: Network daemon not detected — follow the steps below. This page connects automatically once it's running.
onboarding.banner_preview: Preview mode — install the extension to detect the daemon.
